retrieval_summary.note: T1/T2 run: 14 searches, 6 fetches, 8 papers. T5/T6 run: 14 searches, 8 fetches (5 successful), 9 papers.

targets.title: Percolation Threshold Theory x T Cell Infiltration in Solid Tumors
targets.disjointness_notes: Session 015 deep retrieval found Ashworth 2015 (Adv Healthcare Mater) applies percolation analysis to collagen scaffolds for cell invasion — constitutes prior art at conceptual level. However, no paper applies percolation CRITICAL PHENOMENA (diverging ξ, universality class, critical exponents) to T cell infiltration in tumor ECM. Upgraded from DISJOINT to PARTIALLY_EXPLORED.

cross_field_papers.title: Cell Invasion in Collagen Scaffold Architectures Characterized by Percolation Theory
cross_field_papers.relevance: Applies percolation theory to characterize collagen scaffold pore connectivity for connective tissue cell invasion. Uses percolation as a structural measurement tool (percolation diameter). Does NOT apply percolation critical phenomena (exponents, scaling, universality) and does NOT study immune cells or tumor tissue.
cross_field_papers.what_remains_novel: Full percolation physics (p_c as immune exclusion threshold; xi ~ |p-p_c|^(-0.876); universality class; finite-size scaling) applied to T cell infiltration in tumor ECM — not established by Ashworth

adjacent_papers.title: Tumor proliferation and diffusion on percolation clusters
adjacent_papers.relevance: Applies percolation theory to TUMOR CELL migration through ECM — not immune cells. Establishes percolation-tumor biology connection but wrong cellular agent.

adjacent_papers.title: A percolation phase transition controls complement protein coating of surfaces
adjacent_papers.relevance: Percolation governs COMPLEMENT (innate immunity) activation. Establishes percolation-immunity bridge but for complement not T cells.

adjacent_papers.title: Site-Percolation Transition of Run-and-Tumble Particles
adjacent_papers.authors: Saha SK, Banerjee A, Mohanty PK
adjacent_papers.relevance: Active particle percolation in 2D: re-entrant phase behavior, continuously varying critical exponents. Key physics paper for T-cell (active particle) corrections to standard percolation. No biological application.

adjacent_papers.title: Macrophages Restrict Tumor Immune Infiltration by Controlling Collagen Topography
adjacent_papers.relevance: Collagen TOPOLOGY (network connectivity) rather than density controls T cell infiltration via Tcf4-Collagen3 axis. Most relevant recent Field C paper. Does not invoke percolation theory.

field_a_key_papers.title: A percolation phase transition controls complement protein coating of surfaces
field_a_key_papers.note: 2025 Cell paper: percolation + immune system (complement). Breakthrough demonstrating feasibility of percolation-immunology bridge.

field_a_key_papers.title: Percolation of collagen stress in a random network model of the alveolar wall
field_a_key_papers.note: Percolation applied to collagen fiber networks in tissue. Relevant to ECM collagen connectivity as percolation substrate.

field_a_key_papers.title: Assembly of Collagen Matrices as a Phase Transition Revealed by Structural and Rheologic Studies
field_a_key_papers.note: Collagen gelation as a percolation phase transition. Establishes collagen crosslink network follows percolation theory during assembly.

field_a_key_papers.title: Signal percolation within a bacterial community
field_a_key_papers.note: Percolation theory applied to biological signaling through heterogeneous communities.

field_c_key_papers.title: Tumor stiffening reversion through collagen crosslinking inhibition improves T cell migration and anti-PD-1 treatment
field_c_key_papers.note: KEY Field C paper: LOX/collagen crosslinking directly controls T cell infiltration. No percolation framework used.

field_c_key_papers.title: Collagen density regulates the activity of tumor-infiltrating T cells
field_c_key_papers.note: Collagen density threshold controls T cell cytotoxic function. Mentions threshold behavior without quantitative percolation model.

field_c_key_papers.title: Lysyl oxidases: linking structures and immunity in the tumor microenvironment
field_c_key_papers.note: Review of LOX function linking ECM structure and tumor immunity.

field_c_key_papers.title: T-cell infiltration and its regulatory mechanisms in cancers: insights at single-cell resolution
field_c_key_papers.note: Comprehensive single-cell resolution review of T cell infiltration mechanisms.

additional_field_c_papers.title: Macrophages Restrict Tumor Immune Infiltration by Controlling Collagen Topography
additional_field_c_papers.note: KEY NEW 2025 paper: collagen TOPOLOGY controls T cell infiltration via Tcf4-Collagen3 axis. Collagen I (aligned) = excluded; Collagen III (intermingled) = permissive. Exactly the connectivity variable percolation theory describes.

additional_field_c_papers.title: Collagen Fiber Structure Guides 3D Motility of Cytotoxic T Lymphocytes
additional_field_c_papers.authors: Pruitt HC, Xu W et al.
additional_field_c_papers.note: Quantitative MSD of CD8+ T cells in aligned vs. unaligned 3 mg/mL collagen. Anisotropic MSD; MLCK-dependent. Provides MSD framework for testing percolation subdiffusion prediction.

additional_field_c_papers.title: Desmoplastic Stroma Restricts T Cell Extravasation and Mediates Immune Exclusion
additional_field_c_papers.note: FAP+ CAF depletion collapses stroma, restores T cell access. Direct evidence that stroma forms a discrete BARRIER (consistent with percolation threshold, not a gradient).

additional_field_a_papers.title: Site-Percolation Transition of Run-and-Tumble Particles
additional_field_a_papers.authors: Saha SK, Banerjee A, Mohanty PK
additional_field_a_papers.note: Active particle percolation in 2D. Re-entrant phase behavior. Critical exponents vary continuously but scaling function universal. 3D extension for fibrous ECM not yet done.

additional_field_a_papers.title: Active Brownian Particles Moving in a Random Lorentz Gas
additional_field_a_papers.authors: Zeitz M, Wolff K, Stark H
additional_field_a_papers.note: Active particles near percolation density: subdiffusion (alpha~0.66); self-trapping paradox at high activity. 2026 perspective review identifies 3D extension as open question.

additional_field_a_papers.title: Bond and Site Percolation in Three Dimensions
additional_field_a_papers.authors: Wang J, Zhou Z, Zhang W, Garoni TM, Deng Y
additional_field_a_papers.note: Benchmark 3D critical exponents: p_c=0.2488, nu=0.876, beta=0.417. Monte Carlo L=512.

bridge_validation.percolation_applied_to_ECM_network: CONFIRMED — collagen gelation papers (2003); pore size percolation papers; Ashworth 2015
bridge_validation.collagen_TOPOLOGY_controls_T_cell_infilt: CONFIRMED — Fusilier 2025 Science Immunology (strongest recent evidence)
bridge_validation.active_particle_corrections_needed: CONFIRMED — Saha 2024, Zeitz 2017: T cells as active particles will have shifted p_c and modified exponents

gap_analysis: No paper applies bond percolation model to T cell migration through collagen ECM
LOX crosslink density has never been formally mapped to bond occupation probability p
The threshold in stiffness at which T cells stop moving (Kuczek 2019) has not been modeled as percolation critical point p_c
Clinically observed hot/cold tumor binary classification not connected to percolation phase transition
Non-linear improvement from LOX inhibition unexplained — could indicate crossing p_c
Spatial correlation length xi of T cell density fluctuations not measured as function of ECM crosslinking
T cell MSD anomalous diffusion exponent alpha not measured near the exclusion threshold
Universality of critical exponents across tumor types not tested
Finite-size scaling of immune phenotype with tumor/biopsy size not tested
Active particle percolation in 3D fibrous ECM not studied (only 2D lattice models)
most_promising_directions: Continuous BAPN dose-response in KPC tumors: measure T cell infiltration and pore connectivity — prediction: sharp threshold (not linear) at p_c
Spatial correlation analysis of multiplex IHC data: compute xi vs LOX activity — prediction: xi ~ |LOX-LOX_c|^(-0.876)
Live 2-photon MSD measurement at varying BAPN doses — prediction: MSD ~ t^0.696 at critical stiffness
Cross-tumor universality test: same nu = 0.876 in PDAC, breast, NSCLC, prostate
Finite-size scaling: biopsy size dependence of hot/cold classification accuracy

targets.title: Acoustic Filter-Bank Theory x Plant Bioacoustics
targets.field_a: Acoustic engineering — matched-filter detection, parallel filter-bank theory
targets.field_c: Plant biology — mechanosensitive channel biology, ultrasonic emission

adjacent_papers.title: Arabidopsis Leaf Trichomes as Acoustic Antennae
adjacent_papers.relevance: Trichomes as mechanical resonators tuned to insect frequency — frequency-selective behavior described in mechanical language only, no filter-bank theory.

adjacent_papers.title: Ensembles of the leaf trichomes of Arabidopsis thaliana selectively vibrate in the frequency range of its primary insect herbivore
adjacent_papers.relevance: Ensemble shows frequency bands of responsiveness with defined band gaps — structurally identical to filter-bank behavior but not named as such.

field_a_key_papers.title: Cochlear filter bank models (general)
field_a_key_papers.note: Mammalian cochlea modeled as parallel filter bank of ~3500 channels. No application to plants.

field_c_key_papers.title: Arabidopsis Leaf Trichomes as Acoustic Antennae
Ensembles of the leaf trichomes of Arabidopsis thaliana selectively vibrate in the frequency range of its primary insect herbivore
Acoustic radiation force on a long cylinder, and potential sound transduction by tomato trichomes
Cellular transduction of mechanical oscillations in plants by the plasma-membrane mechanosensitive channel MSL10
MSL10 is a high-sensitivity mechanosensor in the tactile sense of the Venus flytrap
field_c_key_papers.note: 2025 Nature Comms: MSL10 as high-sensitivity mechanosensor in fast-acting carnivorous plant.

field_c_key_papers.title: Open structure and gating of the Arabidopsis mechanosensitive ion channel MSL10
field_c_key_papers.note: Cryo-EM structure of MSL10. Heptameric open conformation.

field_c_key_papers.title: An Acoustic Communication Model in Plants
field_c_key_papers.note: First end-to-end acoustic communication framework for plants. Biologically-motivated, not filter-bank engineering approach.

field_c_key_papers.title: Is plant acoustic communication fact or fiction?
field_c_key_papers.note: Critical review questioning adequacy of controls in plant acoustic studies. Important counterpoint.

gap_analysis: No paper formalizes trichome ensemble as parallel filter bank with center frequencies, bandwidths, Q-factors
MSL10 characterized only at sub-10 Hz; gating response in kHz range (matching trichome resonance) never measured
No paper tests whether different trichome size classes selectively activate different MSL/MCA channel subtypes
Matched-filter hypothesis (trichome length distribution statistically matched to herbivore acoustic emission spectrum) never tested
Frequency downconversion mechanism from kHz trichome resonance to Hz-range MSL10 activity not characterized
Signal-to-noise analysis for trichome filter bank vs wind background noise not performed
most_promising_directions: Patch-clamp or GCaMP imaging of MSL/MCA channel activity in trichomes during kHz frequency stimulation (completely absent from literature)
Formal Q-factor and center-frequency analysis of trichome size distribution vs herbivore acoustic emission power spectrum across ecotypes
Test: do trichome mutants (short/sparse trichomes) show shifted frequency sensitivity for herbivore detection compared to wild-type?
targets.key_contradiction: MSL10 characterized at sub-10 Hz (wind) vs trichome resonance at 6-200 kHz (insect). 3 orders-of-magnitude frequency gap between documented channel response and documented trichome resonance remains unresolved. Generator should address this in hypothesis.

t5_disjointness.title: Griffith Fracture Mechanics x Bacterial Cell Wall Failure Under β-Lactam Stress
t5_disjointness.field_a: Materials science — Griffith fracture mechanics, stress intensity factors
t5_disjointness.field_c: Microbiology — peptidoglycan mechanics, β-lactam mechanism of action

cross_field_papers.title: Fracture mechanics modeling of popping event during daughter cell separation
cross_field_papers.relevance: ONLY paper applying fracture mechanics energy release rate G to bacterial PG. Uses FE analysis. Addresses NORMAL DIVISION, not beta-lactam lysis. Does not apply Griffith criterion G >= G_c for catastrophic failure.

bridge_validation.energy_threshold_for_lysis: PARTIAL — Gibbs free energy for MEMBRANE (Auer 2013), not PG fracture mechanics
bridge_validation.fracture_mechanics_applied_to_bacterial_: PARTIAL — energy release rate G for NORMAL DIVISION only (Zhou 2018)

gap_analysis: Griffith criterion G >= G_c has never been applied to PG layer under beta-lactam crosslink loss
Fracture toughness K_IC of PG mesh as function of crosslink density never measured or calculated
PG crosslink defect clusters never modeled as Griffith cracks in pressurized shell
Stress intensity factor K_I at crack tips from clustered crosslink voids never calculated
Lysis threshold predicted from fracture mechanics + crosslink loss rate kinetics not in literature
Whether lytic vs tolerant outcomes reflect subcritical vs supercritical crack growth not explored
t5_disjointness.recommendation: PROCEED as novel — Zhou 2018 confirms feasibility of fracture mechanics for bacterial PG, while confirming the T5 specific application (Griffith criterion for beta-lactam lysis) is genuinely unexplored

t6_disjointness.title: Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy x Gut Microbiome Metabolic State
t6_disjointness.field_c: Gut microbiology — microbiome metabolic activity, dysbiosis detection

cross_field_papers.title: Modelling Human Gut-Microbiome Interactions in a 3D Bioelectronic Platform
cross_field_papers.relevance: EIS in gut microbiome context — BUT measures EPITHELIAL BARRIER INTEGRITY (tight junction resistance), not gut microbiome metabolic state. Uses simple Randles circuit for epithelial response.

cross_field_papers.title: Bacterial Extracellular Electron Transfer Occurs in Mammalian Gut
cross_field_papers.relevance: Proves gut bacteria are electrochemically active in vivo; EET correlates with metabolic rate. Uses CV, not EIS. KEY ENABLING PAPER for T6 bridge.

gap_analysis: EIS frequency sweep as metabolic fingerprint of WHOLE GUT MICROBIOME community metabolic state not found in any paper
Randles circuit parameters (Rct, Cdl, Warburg W) as community-level metabolic activity markers not characterized
EIS distinguishing healthy vs dysbiotic gut microbiome not demonstrated
Despite EET confirmed in vivo (Fleckenstein 2019), no one has developed EIS equivalent circuit model linking Rct to gut microbiome metabolic state
Ingestible EIS capsule performing full frequency sweep across GI segments not yet developed
t6_disjointness.recommendation: PROCEED as novel — partial exploration (gut EET confirmed, EIS used in gut context for barrier, EIS encodes metabolic state in biofilm) strengthens feasibility but the core bridge (community metabolic fingerprint) is genuinely unexplored

t3_disjointness.title: Classical Nucleation Theory x Ferroptosis LIP Dynamics
t3_disjointness.field_a: Physics — classical nucleation theory, heterogeneous nucleation kinetics
t3_disjointness.field_c: Cell death biology — ferroptosis, labile iron pool regulation

adjacent_papers.title: Modeling the iron storage protein ferritin reveals how residual ferrihydrite iron determines initial ferritin iron sequestration kinetics
adjacent_papers.relevance: Ferritin iron core formation modeled kinetically — but uses empirical rate laws, NOT CNT. Explicitly states nucleation mechanism unknown. No connection to ferroptosis or LIP.

adjacent_papers.title: Activation of lysosomal iron triggers ferroptosis in cancer
adjacent_papers.relevance: 2025 Nature paper on lysosomal iron mechanism. No nucleation or CNT content. Confirms iron threshold question open.

adjacent_papers.title: Labile iron pool dynamics do not drive ferroptosis potentiation in colorectal cancer cells
adjacent_papers.relevance: KEY ANOMALY: LIP did not expand during ferroptosis induction — contradicts standard model. Exactly the puzzle CNT stochastic threshold analysis could address.

field_a_key_papers.title: Observation of the Assembly of the Nascent Mineral Core at the Nucleation Site of Human Mitochondrial Ferritin
field_a_key_papers.note: Two-step nucleation in ferritin iron core assembly. Relevant CNT extension in the biological context adjacent to the bridge.

field_a_key_papers.title: Classical and Nonclassical Nucleation and Growth Mechanisms for Nanoparticle Formation
field_a_key_papers.note: Review of CNT and nonclassical alternatives. Framework for the bridge hypothesis.

field_c_key_papers.title: Activation of lysosomal iron triggers ferroptosis in cancer
A guide to ferroptosis, the biological rust of cellular membranes
field_c_key_papers.note: 2024 FEBS review: LIP threshold explicitly identified as open problem.

field_c_key_papers.title: Labile iron pool dynamics do not drive ferroptosis potentiation in colorectal cancer cells

t3_disjointness.key_anomaly: Srivastava 2025 (PMID 40631145): LIP does not expand during GPX4 inhibition-initiated ferroptosis in colorectal cancer cells — challenges standard model. CNT stochastic analysis could explain: localized nucleation events in individual lysosomes, not bulk LIP changes, trigger Fenton chemistry.
gap_analysis: Zero papers found applying CNT to ferrihydrite dissolution or LIP overflow threshold
Ferritin iron sequestration model (Harrison 2023) explicitly avoids CNT due to unknown nucleation mechanism — gap
Reverse heterogeneous nucleation of ferrihydrite has no physical chemistry framework
Stochastic first-passage time analysis of lysosomal iron release not in any paper
Cell-type variation in ferroptosis susceptibility not explained by ferrihydrite particle size (CNT-derived Kelvin equation prediction untested)
LIP threshold paradox (Srivastava 2025) has no mechanistic resolution in current literature
t3_disjointness.recommendation: PROCEED — DISJOINT with confidence 0.97. The Srivastava 2025 LIP paradox is a specific, resolvable puzzle that makes this bridge scientifically actionable, not merely speculative.

t4_disjointness.title: Linear Viscoelastic Creep Theory x Biofilm Antibiotic Tolerance
t4_disjointness.field_a: Materials science — linear viscoelastic theory, Maxwell/Kelvin-Voigt models, creep compliance
t4_disjointness.field_c: Microbiology — biofilm antibiotic tolerance, persister formation, EPS matrix
t4_disjointness.scout_note: Scout noted 'biofilm rheology subfield exists' — this was correct. Specific bridge absent as Scout suspected.

cross_field_papers.title: Viscoelasticity of biofilms and their recalcitrance to mechanical and chemical challenges
cross_field_papers.relevance: Maxwell elements in parallel quantitatively linked to chlorhexidine penetration. Closest to bridge but uses Maxwell relaxation elements, not J(t) formalism. No G'/G'' crossover for MIC prediction.

cross_field_papers.title: Effect of biofilm physical characteristics on their susceptibility to antibiotics: impacts of low-frequency ultrasound
cross_field_papers.relevance: Creep compliance J(t) formula explicitly used. Power-law exponent alpha classifies elastic vs viscous. Correlates compliance with tobramycin diffusion. No formal constitutive model linking J(t) to drug diffusion rate.

cross_field_papers.title: Biofilm structure as a key factor in antibiotic tolerance: insights from Bacillus subtilis model systems
cross_field_papers.relevance: Direct correlation between G'/G'' and daptomycin tolerance (3-log difference). No formal creep theory or MIC prediction model.

cross_field_papers.title: Alginate exopolymer significantly modulates the viscoelastic properties and resilience of bacterial biofilms
cross_field_papers.relevance: Alginate EPS modulates G'/G''; mesh size connects EPS composition to drug penetration. No J(t) formalism.

cross_field_papers.title: N-Acetyl-Cysteine Increases Activity of Gold Nanoparticles Against Pseudomonas aeruginosa Biofilms
cross_field_papers.relevance: Creep compliance decreases with nanoparticle + NAC treatment; synergistic tobramycin effect. No formal J(t) model.

cross_field_papers.title: Repurposing DNase I and alginate lyase to degrade biofilm matrix combined with antibiotics
cross_field_papers.relevance: Correlation between elasticity/viscosity reduction and treatment efficacy in mucoid P. aeruginosa biofilms.

t4_disjointness.key_contradiction: Beyond-matrix vs. matrix-centric models (2025): 'Beyond the matrix' paper argues metabolic adaptation is primary tolerance mechanism in CF biofilms — contradicts Lieleg 2015, Gloag 2025, Wu 2025 treating viscoelastic barrier as primary. This contradiction is itself an opportunity: the Generator can propose that creep compliance predicts WHICH mechanism dominates in a given biofilm.
gap_analysis: J(t) = J_0 + J_1(1-exp(-t/tau)) + t/eta never used as governing equation for antibiotic concentration profile in biofilm
G'/G'' crossover frequency as quantitative MIC/MBEC predictor absent from all literature
Retardation spectrum H(tau) not applied to predict distribution of antibiotic penetration timescales
Creep compliance parameters (J_0, J_1, tau, eta) not used to derive pharmacokinetic model for biofilm drug penetration
Maxwell relaxation time constants not linked to persister cell formation or spatial distribution
G'/G'' crossover as percolation transition in EPS network for antibiotic accessibility not explored
t4_disjointness.recommendation: PROCEED — PARTIALLY EXPLORED at hard end. The empirical correlation substrate exists; the mathematical formalism (formal creep constitutive law applied to drug transport; gel point as MIC threshold) is absent. Hypothesis should be clearly framed as formalizing and quantifying the existing empirical observations, not as introducing a completely new connection.
